claims.source_quote: Transcription factors (TFs) regulate gene expression through complex and co-operative mechanisms.

claims.text: Transcription factors regulate gene expression through complex and co-operative mechanisms.
claims.source_quote: Transcription factors (TFs) regulate gene expression through complex and co-operative mechanisms.
While many TFs act together, the logic underlying TFs binding and their interactions is not fully understood yet.

claims.text: The logic underlying TF binding and TF interactions is not fully understood.
claims.source_quote: the logic underlying TFs binding and their interactions is not fully understood yet.

claims.text: Most current approaches for TF binding site prediction focus on individual TFs.
claims.source_quote: Most current approaches for TF binding site prediction focus on individual TFs and binary classification tasks, without a full analysis of the possible interactions among various TFs.

claims.text: Most current approaches for TF binding site prediction are formulated as binary classification tasks.
claims.source_quote: Most current approaches for TF binding site prediction focus on individual TFs and binary classification tasks, without a full analysis of the possible interactions among various TFs.

claims.text: Most current approaches for TF binding site prediction do not perform a full analysis of possible interactions among various TFs.
claims.source_quote: Most current approaches for TF binding site prediction focus on individual TFs and binary classification tasks, without a full analysis of the possible interactions among various TFs.

claims.text: The authors investigate DNA TF binding site recognition as a multi-label classification problem.
claims.source_quote: In this paper we investigate DNA TF binding site recognition as a multi-label classification problem, achieving reliable predictions for multiple TFs on DNA sequences retrieved in public repositories.

claims.text: The DNA sequences used in this study were retrieved from public repositories.
claims.source_quote: achieving reliable predictions for multiple TFs on DNA sequences retrieved in public repositories.

claims.text: The deep learning models used in this study are based on Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCNs).
claims.source_quote: Our deep learning models are based on Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCNs), which are able to predict multiple TF binding profiles, capturing correlations among TFs and their cooperative regulatory mechanisms.

claims.text: Temporal Convolutional Networks are able to predict multiple TF binding profiles.
claims.source_quote: Our deep learning models are based on Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCNs), which are able to predict multiple TF binding profiles, capturing correlations among TFs and their cooperative regulatory mechanisms.

claims.text: Temporal Convolutional Networks capture correlations among TFs and their cooperative regulatory mechanisms.
claims.source_quote: Our deep learning models are based on Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCNs), which are able to predict multiple TF binding profiles, capturing correlations among TFs and their cooperative regulatory mechanisms.

claims.text: Multi-label learning leads to reliable predictive performances for TF binding site prediction.
claims.source_quote: Our results suggest that multi-label learning leading to reliable predictive performances can reveal biologically meaningful motifs and co-binding patterns consistent with known TF interactions.

claims.text: Multi-label learning can reveal biologically meaningful motifs consistent with known TF interactions.
claims.source_quote: Our results suggest that multi-label learning leading to reliable predictive performances can reveal biologically meaningful motifs and co-binding patterns consistent with known TF interactions, while also suggesting novel relationships and cooperation among TFs.

claims.text: Multi-label learning can reveal co-binding patterns consistent with known TF interactions.
claims.source_quote: Our results suggest that multi-label learning leading to reliable predictive performances can reveal biologically meaningful motifs and co-binding patterns consistent with known TF interactions, while also suggesting novel relationships and cooperation among TFs.

claims.text: The multi-label learning approach suggests novel relationships and cooperation among TFs.
